scope_boundary: Public-safe local external Circle MultiCoil phase-feature receipt import only. The slice records one pinned `CC-AI-CONTRACT-PHASE-FEATURE-001` structural phase fixture at Circle commit `63b0f511`, `joint_repeat_horizon=35`, `relative_phase=3`, `shifted_relative_phase=3`, five required theorem IDs, two planner recommendations, contract fingerprint `4b562beab64ec863903e4267f50c90049f0d3fa612f6c1bb2f06ad07e821ffd7`, command-output digests, targeted Circle CLI test output `3 passed in 2.99s`, and targeted contract-ready recommendation test output `1 passed in 1.76s`. It does not prove MultiCoil, RoPE, attention, retrieval, model quality, context length, runtime speed, memory scaling, hardware efficiency, training stability, deployment readiness, transfer, benchmark performance, ASI, or CoilRA chapter-core promotion.
transition_reason: The MultiCoil phase receipt slice makes one Circle phase-feature structural fixture concrete in the book and closes one stale 'no MultiCoil phase receipt import' blocker for the CoilRA chapter. The evidence remains an external-project receipt import with no ASI-side RoPE or MultiCoil benchmark, no ordinary position-bucket, learned-position, RoPE, dense-attention, recurrent, or state-space baselines with workload metrics, no hardware-kernel measurement, no attention/retrieval/model-quality result, and no independent review, so it blocks promotion rather than moving support state.
